rulings.Case Title: OLIVER HILSENRATH et al VS. MIKE SHEPARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution , Or, In The Alternative, For Order Requiring Plaintiff Oliver Hilsenrath To Commence Arbitration Within 30 Days
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT MICHAEL SHEPARD Notice of Renewed Motion to Dismiss for Delay in Prosecution, or, in the Alternative, for Order Requiring Plaintiff Oliver Hilsenrath to Commence Arbitration within thirty Days. Motion denied without prejudice upon properly noticed petition, the court would consider setting an arbitration completion date. (CCP section 1283.8.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Demurrer To First, Second, Third, Fourth, And Fifth Causes Of Action In Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. Demurrer to First, Second, Third, Fourth, and Fifth Causes of Action in Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend. As to the first cause of action, violation of the CLRA. Plaintiffs must plead representations beyond mere "puffery" or that relate directly to the alleged defect. As to the second cause of action, violation of the UCL, plaintiff must allege with particularity the fraudulent business practice or the violation of law. The third cause of action for fraudulent concealment/nondisclosure lacks specificity. The complaint does not adequately allege a serious personal safety risk that plaintiffs, or any identifiable class member, experienced. As to the fourth cause of action for breach of express warranty, no claim is stated because latent defects that are discovered after express warranty expiration are not covered. Dougherty vs. American Honda (2006) 144 Cal.App. 4th 824. The fifth cause of action for unjust enrichment is dependent upon the survival of another cause of action. The Court would not find any of these claims to be time-barred for purposes of demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Moot, in view of the court's ruling on demurrer. Issues may be re-raised. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: M. TODD JENKS VS. DLA PIPER RUDNICK GRAY CARY US LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration; Points And Authorities , Declarations Of David J. Reis And Lisa Partridge In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT DLA PIPER LLP (US) Petition to Compel Arbitration. Petition to compel arbitration granted. The Termination agreement is a separate agreement applying to issues of termination. The parties clearly entered into an arbitration agreement in the employment offer letter. There is a presumption in favor of arbitration, and any doubts concerning the scope of arbitratable issues should be resolved in favor of arbitration. See Cione vs. Foresters Equity Services, Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App. 4th 625.=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY VS. BURT HAMROL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANTS BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY, CHRISTOPHER GREENE, MARK MCDONALD DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to breach of written contract. Hamrol places a clearly erroneous interpretation on the written terms of the construction loan agreement. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to breach of oral contract regarding Greene. Demurrer overruled as to remainder. Hamrol's fraud allegations do not directly vary with the terms of the written contract and therefore, the fraud exception to the parole evidence rule can apply. Overruled as to money had and received. Paragraph 13 alleges transfer of money to Borel. Cross-complainant shall file amended cross-complaint to redraft breach of oral contract cause of action to conform with the court's ruling. Cross-complainant may not add any additional causes of action without court's approval. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS BOCO BORJA VS. CITIFINANCIAL SERVICES INC DBA CITIFINANCIAL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT CITIFINANCIAL SERVICES INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must clearly allege that the letters sent by defendant to plaintiff were in regard to the account at issue. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER SOLUTIONS LLC VS. JOSE DAVID,JR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 15, 2010, line 8, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER SOLUTIONS LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof that valid foreclosure sale occurred. Trustee's Deed Upon Sale reference public auction occurring on August 25, 2009. Notice of Trustee's Sale states date of sale is July 21, 2009. No evidence presented that the August 25, 2009 sale was noticed. =(302/CWW)
